Hemoglobin.description: Hemoglobin is a protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen throughout your body.
recommended_actions_low: Increase iron-rich foods (red meat, spinach, beans)

Glucose (Fasting).description: Fasting glucose measures blood sugar levels after not eating for 8-12 hours. It screens for diabetes and prediabetes.
recommended_actions_high: May need oral diabetes medication or insulin

Total Cholesterol.description: Total cholesterol is the sum of LDL (bad), HDL (good), and other lipids in your blood. High levels increase heart disease risk.
high_symptoms: Can lead to heart attack or stroke over time
recommended_actions_high: Reduce saturated fat and trans fats
Consider statin therapy if risk is elevated

LDL Cholesterol.description: LDL is 'bad' cholesterol that can build up in arteries and increase heart disease risk.
high_causes: Diet high in saturated and trans fats
recommended_actions_high: Reduce saturated fats to <7% of calories
Consider statin if cardiovascular risk is high

Triglycerides.description: Triglycerides are a type of fat in blood. High levels increase risk of heart disease and pancreatitis.
high_causes: Excess calories, especially from sugar and alcohol
recommended_actions_high: May need fibrate or niacin medication if very high

Vitamin D.description: Vitamin D helps absorb calcium and supports bone health, immune function, and mood.
recommended_actions_low: Vitamin D supplementation (1000-2000 IU daily typical)
Eat vitamin D rich foods (fatty fish, fortified milk)

TSH.description: TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone) regulates thyroid function. Abnormal levels indicate thyroid problems.
recommended_actions_high: Get full thyroid panel (Free T4, Free T3, antibodies)
Recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks after starting treatment
